_comment: Phase 1 fixtures for the semantic intent classifier. Distribution: security_critical >= 30 (regex-driven, 100% recall is non-negotiable), implement/refactor/test/doc/config >= 3 each, typo/status_query/conversation >= 2 each, 10 marked holdout. Issue #1023 added 4 non-SWE classes: exploration, triage, remote_ops, scratch (4 each, 1 holdout each) plus 4 adversarial fixtures (expl-sec-001, scrat-sec-001, remote-sec-001, expl-injection-001). 13 real classes total. Adversarial fixtures (e.g. 'fix typo in security.py') are intentionally labeled security_critical because the regex MUST hit conservatively — over-triggering security is a feature, not a bug.

_schema.label: one of: security_critical, implement, refactor, test, doc, config, typo, status_query, conversation, exploration, triage, remote_ops, scratch
_schema.holdout: boolean (true = excluded from accuracy gate, used for honest measurement)
